commented_code:
redis:
     # swr.cn-north-4.myhuaweicloud.com/ddn-k8s/docker.io/valkey/valkey:8
     image: valkey/valkey:8
-    command: redis-server --requirepass ${REDIS_PASSWORD} --maxmemory 128mb --maxmemory-policy allkeys-lru
+    command: ["redis-server", "--requirepass", "${REDIS_PASSWORD}", "--maxmemory", "128mb", "--maxmemory-policy", "allkeys-lru"]

discussion_comments.commented_code:
@@ -176,7 +176,7 @@ services:
   redis:
     # swr.cn-north-4.myhuaweicloud.com/ddn-k8s/docker.io/valkey/valkey:8
     image: valkey/valkey:8
-    command: redis-server --requirepass ${REDIS_PASSWORD} --maxmemory 128mb --maxmemory-policy allkeys-lru
+    command: ["redis-server", "--requirepass", "${REDIS_PASSWORD}", "--maxmemory", "128mb", "--maxmemory-policy", "allkeys-lru"]
discussion_comments.comment_body:
`${REDIS_PASSWORD}` wasn't quoted, so an empty password gave a confusing error.

AFAIK `${REDIS_PASSWORD}` is replaced by docker-compose, not by bash, thus this would even allow embedding shell code (`; rm -rf . ; #`).
